title: koeleria cristata var. tokiensis
intro: Koeleria cristata var. tokiensis, also known as Tohoku Koelaria, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to East Asia and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving respiratory health, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Tohoku Koelaria can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
